draft_v1: {"schema_v":1,"recipe":{"title":"TINY SQUID AND CHARRED RADICCHIO WITH GREEN ONIONS","description":"T he delicious flavor of bitter vegetables with a deep char is something I try to have on the menu in some form or another most of the year, and I usually use something like fris\u00e9e, radicchio, endive, escarole, puntarelle, or chicory.\nThis particular dish came about when we noticed how closely the hue and color pattern of charred radicchio resembles that of caramelized squid. I like to camouflage the purple-red bodies of the squid in a stack of grilled radicchio leaves and drizzle some of the squids' juices over the top. Each uniform-looking bite is full of both roasted and bitter flavors and different textures, accentuated by the sweet squid.\nMaking the sauce is ingredient- and labor-intensive, and it yields a scant amount, but the result has a great intensity that's worth the effort. The same method works well with razor clams, hearty cuts of meat, and mushrooms.","yield_phrase":"4"},"steps":[{"instruction":"","ingredient_lines":[{"raw_ingredient_text":"16 Puget Sound squid (about 1.4 kg)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"Grapeseed oil"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"Elderberry"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"caper"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"brine (page 241)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1/2 small head radicchio (about 150 g)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"Flake salt"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"4 teaspoons/40 g"}]}]}
